Update README and tests for chat feature and config paths

- Add chat command, --compact, --days, --before, --notify to README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add multi-scenario runner for batch pipeline testing

New `pingu.scenarios` package with:
- ScenarioSpec: dataclass for scenario parameters
- ScenarioRunner: batch execution with per-scenario progress
- ScenarioReport: ASCII summary table, stats by modulation/SNR, CSV export, comparison plots
- CLI (`scripts/run_scenarios.py`): quick sweep via flags or YAML manifest
- Example manifest (`configs/scenarios/example_sweep.yaml`)
- 22 tests covering spec expansion, YAML loading, runner, and report

Usage:
  uv run python scripts/run_scenarios.py --modulations all --snr-start 0 --snr-stop 30 --snr-step 10 --plot
  uv run python scripts/run_scenarios.py --manifest configs/scenarios/example_sweep.yaml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Multiply agent flow to busy digital highway — 3-4 lanes per route

Each of the 26 cultural arc routes now spawns 3-4 parallel agent lanes
(~90 total lanes vs 26 before). Each lane runs at a different height
offset (+/-0.03) and speed (0.6-1.1x), with 25-40 particles per lane.

Total particles: ~2,800 vs ~1,040 before (nearly 3x).
Stronger arcs (strength > 0.7) get 4 lanes, others get 3.

Visual contrast: State 3 = graceful rivers (26 warm cream arcs, 20
particles each). State 4 = busy digital highway (90 cyan lanes, 30
particles each, 2-3x faster). The density difference IS the story.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Strategy Not Found, concurrent queue, remove Contabo, clean logs, add validation

- Fix Strategy Not Found: resolve Supabase UUID to numeric user ID with caching
- Register StrategyController/StrategyService in BacktestModule (was dead code)
- Add GET :id/details endpoint for strategy details with runs/backtests
- Rewrite queue: N concurrent workers with DB-level FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
- Remove all hardcoded Contabo IP references and data-server dependency
- Remove ~37 console.log/error statements for clean production logs
- Add DTO input validation: bounds on all numeric fields, array size limits
- Dynamic backtest timeout (5-15min) based on payload complexity

commit.message:
Switch to Outlook IMAP for collecting Substack posts

Substack blocks RSS/API requests from datacenter IPs (Cloudflare).
Instead, read Substack emails from Outlook inbox via IMAP and use
Outlook SMTP for sending the digest. Removes RSS/API collector,
Gmail config, and SUBSTACK_SID dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: nettoyage fichier avatar orphelin si l'UPDATE DB échoue

Avant : le fichier était écrit sur disque puis, si le UPDATE users
échouait, il restait orphelin indéfiniment.

Après : ajout de std::fs::remove_file dans tous les chemins d'erreur
survenant après la création du fichier :
- erreur de lecture de chunk multipart
- fichier trop volumineux (> 20 Mo)
- erreur d'écriture / tokio block
- échec du UPDATE SQL (cas principal signalé par la review)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: format selector 301/300/bestvideo+bestaudio/best

ISP proxy returns HLS (301/300) formats only. The ext=mp4/m4a
filters excluded HLS streams causing "format not available".
New selector tries 301 and 300 (HLS) first, then DASH fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: Remove Vizard API, Cloudflare R2, and download:clip code from main process

Remove all Vizard and R2 dependencies and handlers (S3Client, Upload imports,
vizardRequest helper, 5 Vizard IPC handlers, r2:upload handler, download:clip
handler). Remove vizardAccountId from platform objects. Replace r2Config,
vizardApiKey, vizardProjects, uploadedFiles store defaults with outputFolder,
sfxFolder, whisperModel, localProjects for the new local editing pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add WebGL status indicator and improve control panel UX

- Add real-time WebGL instance counter with visual status indicator
- Show green/yellow status badge based on WebGL availability
- Add info text explaining CSS vs WebGL glass differences
- Add active indicator dot on WebGL tab when instances are running
- Improve user feedback when WebGL is not yet initialized
- Poll for WebGL instances every second to update status

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Disable Workbox precaching to fix stale PWA on mobile

Precaching stored all JS/CSS/HTML in Cache Storage, causing phones to
serve months-old assets. Caddy already handles caching correctly
(immutable for hashed assets, no-cache for HTML/sw.js).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add "When to Use / When NOT to Use" sections to all 38 lessons

Every lesson now has explicit guidance on when each technique is
effective and when it can backfire. This addresses the gap between
knowing a technique and having the discernment to apply it well.
Intent alone isn't enough — context, timing, and judgment matter.

commit.message:
Fix: undgå duplikat-virksomheder i HubSpot ved at sætte domain-felt

HubSpot opretter automatisk en virksomhed fra email-domænet (itfs.com)
ved siden af vores manuelt oprettede virksomhed (ITFS) — to duplikater.

Fix: upsertHubspotCompany søger nu på domain før navn, og sætter
domain-feltet ved oprettelse. Når HubSpot's auto-association derefter
ser itfs.com, finder den den eksisterende virksomhed i stedet for at
oprette en ny.

Søgerækkefølge: domain → navn → opret ny (med domain sat)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(youtube-listener-innertube): add jitter on startup to prevent YouTube 429 rate limiting

When pods restart with 20+ channels, all goroutines simultaneously fetch the
YouTube watch page causing mass 429 rate-limit responses. Add a random 0-5s
jitter before each channel's first watch page request to spread the load.

commit.message:
Add token budget guard with --compact flag for chat context

Cap each section of the system prompt to prevent blowing past small
model context windows: bandwidth (15/10), known devices (50/25),
network snapshots (200/50), mesh snapshots (200/50) for full/compact
modes. The --compact flag selects tighter limits for smaller models.

Fix semgrep security findings: path traversal and XSS suppressions

- db/migrate-data.js: add path.basename() to loadJSON to prevent path
  traversal if filename arg is ever user-controlled
- public/app.js: separate innerHTML assignments onto their own lines and
  add nosemgrep suppressions for 7 false-positive XSS findings where all
  user data is already escaped via escapeHtml/escapeHtmlAttribute, or
  content is from hardcoded lookup tables with no user data injected

security(auth): block legacy admin login in production

The shared admin password path is now blocked in production environments
(SARDIS_ENVIRONMENT=production|prod). It remains available in dev/staging
for backward compatibility with a Q3 2026 sunset date. This isolates the
legacy auth path without breaking CI/staging workflows.

commit.message:
Complete documentation overhaul (#301)

* Complete documentation overhaul with 25 new/rewritten pages (#301)

Rewrites the entire documentation site from scratch to match the current
library capabilities (get_acs, get_pums, get_decennial, get_estimates,
get_flows, MOE utilities, spatial support, survey design, caching, CLI).

- Upgrade to mkdocs-material theme with tabs, code copy, admonitions
- Rewrite README with hero examples, badges, and feature overview
- Add Getting Started section: installation, quickstart, Census 101
- Add 12 topic guides: ACS, Decennial, PUMS, Estimates, Flows,
  Variables, Geography, MOE, Spatial, Survey Design, Caching, Multi-year
- Add API reference with mkdocstrings, CLI reference, datasets reference
- Add 3 migration guides: from tidycensus, from old PyPUMS, from FTP
- Add troubleshooting sections across guides
- Add cross-links between all guides and references
- Remove outdated user-guide/, history.md, surveys.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address all PR review feedback

- Add content.code.annotate feature flag to mkdocs.yml (critical)
- Add mkdocs-autorefs as explicit docs dependency in pyproject.toml
- Update pyproject.toml description to reflect current library scope
- Align example years from 2022 to 2023 in index.md and README.md
- Add lookup_fips() tip in from-tidycensus.md for county FIPS lookup
- Add info admonition explaining NaN moe values in acs-data.md

commit.message:
Refactor substitute route: pre-filter DB candidates by movement pattern

Instead of asking AI to generate exercise names (fragile, can pick wrong
category), now query the DB for valid candidates first and ask AI to select
from that list. Pipeline:

1. Map workout day type → allowed movementPatterns via ALLOWED_PATTERNS constant
   (pull→pull/hinge, push→push, legs→squat/hinge/carry, etc.)
2. Query exercises matching those patterns + overlapping primary muscles (pass 1)
   Widen to all allowed-pattern exercises if < 3 results (pass 2)
3. Strip candidates whose injuryFlags match user's active conditions (code layer)
4. AI selects + ranks from pre-validated list — exerciseIds are in the prompt
5. Hydrate response from candidateMap — no second DB round-trip

AI can no longer suggest an exercise from the wrong movement category because
wrong-category exercises are structurally absent from the candidate list.
Also reduces max_tokens (600→400) and temperature (0.7→0.5) since the task
is now selection/ranking, not open-ended generation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add aria-label to all icon-only buttons for accessibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove duplicate detection from clean_obsolete.py

Completes separation of concerns between the two scripts:
- clean_duplicates.py: Fast MD5-based duplicate detection
- clean_obsolete.py: Content-based obsolete file detection

Removed:
- load_checksums_from_cache() method
- MD5 checksum tracking in scan_folder/scan_drive
- Duplicate detection logic in analyze_files()
- Duplicate filtering in content analysis
- duplicates_found stat from reports
- md5Checksum from API query fields

This ensures clean_obsolete.py only finds obsolete files (old, temp,
backup) and never suggests duplicates for deletion.

Fix event zone rendering crash and rework sea-area zone system

The expandedHull variable was referenced but never defined in the zone
label positioning code, causing a ReferenceError that broke drawMap()
whenever a snow/fog/gale event was active. Replaced convex hull approach
with per-milepost circle rendering and multi-source BFS from coastal
mileposts for accurate sea-area zones.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: strip whitespace from BASE_URL to prevent newline in stream URL

BASE_URL env var on Render had a trailing newline. .rstrip("/") only
strips slashes so the newline survived into the wss:// WebSocket URL
sent to Twilio, causing Twilio to immediately drop the call. Changed
to .strip().rstrip("/") to remove all leading/trailing whitespace first.

wrangler.toml contains CF resource IDs (D1 database, KV namespace) that
should not be public. Follows the same .dev.vars / .dev.vars.example
pattern already in the project. Real IDs restored locally; example file
has placeholders with create instructions.

commit.message:
Improve spot detection with HSV hue/saturation channels and bigger labels

Detection improvements:
- Add HSV hue and saturation difference channels alongside grayscale
- Run grayscale detection at multiple sensitivity levels (1x, 0.7x, 0.5x)
- Lower minimum area/circularity thresholds to catch more subtle spots
- Smarter size filter: gap-based separation finds natural break between
  artifacts (r=10-15) and real spots (r=20+)

Label improvements:
- Font size scales with image (1.5% of height, ~45px for 3000px images)
- Thicker spot circles and zone boundaries
- Larger center dots

commit.message:
ci: add -lws2_32 to Windows FFmpeg link flags for Tesseract

Tesseract's libtesseract.a uses Windows sockets (WSAStartup etc.)
but lists ws2_32 as a PRIVATE CMake dependency, so it doesn't appear
in tesseract.pc's Libs.private. Add -lws2_32 explicitly to the
Windows extra-ldflags to resolve the symbols at static link time.

commit.message:
Auto-assign default top type on new templates and store tops in global consensus DB

New templates auto-assign the business's default top type (isDefault: true) when
none is explicitly chosen. TareWeightEditModal pre-selects the default for new bottles.

MasterProduct now stores defaultTopCategory (cap/cork/screw_cap/pour_spout/none)
and defaultTopWeightG from aggregated observations. TareObservation accepts
topCategory and topWeightG on contribution. Consensus recalculation computes
top category by weighted mode and top weight by weighted mean.

Global tare suggestions now include top type data. Accepting a suggestion
auto-matches to the closest business top type by category keyword. Template
creation/update auto-contributes top data to the global DB when sharing is enabled.

commit.message:
feat: add search_keywords table and CRUD API, fetch keywords from DB in serpapi collector

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: bypass FunctionsClient — use raw fetch in invokeFunction with explicit JWT

The Supabase FunctionsClient only calls setAuth() on SIGNED_IN/TOKEN_REFRESHED
events, not on INITIAL_SESSION (session restored from storage). This caused
invoke() to send the anon key instead of the user JWT, resulting in "Invalid JWT"
errors from the edge function.

Fix: replace supabase.functions.invoke() with a raw fetch() call. JWT is read
fresh from supabase.auth.getSession() on every invokeFunction call, guaranteeing
the correct token is always used regardless of auth event lifecycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
